memes.label: Mr. Ackfield
memes.description: A Plymouth-based lawyer who represents the conspirators' legal interests in the Cheyne mystery.

memes.label: Alwyn Road
memes.description: A crossroad adjacent to Hopefield Avenue, where Cheyne conducted his surveillance of the criminal gang.

memes.label: "Antwerp"
memes.description: "The Belgian port city that serves as the primary destination of Inspector French's continental investigation and the location of the shipping firm Merkel & Lowenthal."

memes.label: "Arnold Price"
memes.description: "Arnold Price is the creator of the cipher at the heart of The Cheyne Mystery — a junior naval officer who encoded Otto Schulz's secret before his death."

memes.label: "Arnold Price's Cipher"
memes.description: "The sealed envelope containing a coded document, left by Arnold Price to Maxwell Cheyne, which held the key to a buried German submarine treasure."

memes.label: Arthur Lamson
memes.description: A key antagonist who drugged and abducted Maxwell Cheyne near Plymouth, later revealed to be Arnold Price's cousin and the mastermind behind the kidnapping plot.

memes.label: Baker Street
memes.description: A major London street and underground station, from which Cheyne and Joan departed for Wembley Park.

memes.label: "Belgium"
memes.description: "The country that serves as the primary setting for Inspector French's continental investigation in The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: "Blessington"
memes.description: "The primary antagonist of The Cheyne Mystery, who uses multiple aliases and leads the conspiracy to obtain Arnold Price's secret document."

memes.label: "Bombay"
memes.description: "The Indian port city where Arnold Price disappeared from his ship, marking a crucial turning point in the novel's backstory."

memes.label: Brixham
memes.description: Small fishing port at the northeast corner of the Dartmouth peninsula, the first town searched by Cheyne in his pursuit of the Enid.

memes.label: "Bruges"
memes.description: "The Belgian city where Inspector French traces the conspirators' movements via a hotel bill found among the cipher documents."

memes.label: Brussels
memes.description: Capital of Belgium, used by Inspector French as a transit point and investigative base during the continental phase of the case.

memes.label: Captain Amery
memes.description: A sea captain who commanded the pursuit vessel and provided crucial nautical expertise during the final chase in the English Channel.

memes.label: Captain Cheyne
memes.description: The late father of Maxwell Cheyne, a navy commander who passed on his love of the sea and adventure to his son.

memes.label: Characters Index
memes.description: Complete index of all characters appearing in The Cheyne Mystery by Freeman Wills Crofts.

memes.label: "Colton Street"
memes.description: "A street in Putney, London, home to Mr. Sproule at No. 17 and William Sime at No. 12"

memes.label: Concepts Index
memes.description: Index of all key concepts, ciphers, vessels, and thematic elements in The Cheyne Mystery.

memes.label: "Dalton Road"
memes.description: "A road in a London suburb, location of the gang's second hideout after they evacuated Laurel Lodge"

memes.label: "James Dangle"
memes.description: "James Dangle is the primary antagonist in The Cheyne Mystery, a criminal conspirator who orchestrates the theft of the cipher tracing and the abduction of Joan Merrill."

memes.label: "Dartmouth"
memes.description: "The Devonshire port town near Warren Lodge, Maxwell Cheyne's home, and the setting for key events in the early chapters of The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: "Earlswood"
memes.description: "Secluded house on Dalton Road, Wembley, used as the gang's second hideout after evacuating Hopefield Avenue in The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: "Edgecombe Hotel"
memes.description: "The hotel in Plymouth where Maxwell Cheyne is drugged by Blessington, setting the mystery in motion."

memes.label: Events Index
memes.description: Chronological index of all key events in The Cheyne Mystery by Freeman Wills Crofts.

memes.label: "Exeter"
memes.description: "The Devonshire city that serves as a key railway junction during the conspirators' train pursuit of Maxwell Cheyne."

memes.label: Fenchurch Street
memes.description: A street in the City of London where the shipping firm that employed young Maxwell Cheyne was located.

memes.label: Frau Schulz
memes.description: Widow of U-boat captain Otto Schulz, beneficiary of the secret treasure discovered by her late husband.

memes.label: Mr. Hackworth
memes.description: A stout gentleman and garage proprietor interviewed by Inspector French during the investigation, who provided critical information about the yellow car.

memes.label: "Harwich"
memes.description: "Port town in Essex, England, serving as the departure point for the Harwich-Zeebrugge ferry to Belgium in The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: "Hopefield Avenue"
memes.description: "A residential street in the suburbs of London where Laurel Lodge is located, central to the early events of the story"

memes.label: "Horne Terrace"
memes.description: "A block of workers' flats in Burton Street, Chelsea, London — Joan Merrill's residence and a key location in the investigation"

memes.label: Mr. Horton
memes.description: Co-founder of Horton and Lavender's Private Detective Agency, deceased before the events of the story.

memes.label: "Horton and Lavender's Detective Agency"
memes.description: "Horton and Lavender's Private Detective Agency, the London firm employing Mr. Speedwell, engaged by Maxwell Cheyne to investigate the conspirators."

memes.label: Hubert Parkes
memes.description: A guest at the Edgecombe Hotel whose name appeared as a visitor to Maxwell Cheyne, later linked to the hotel incident.

memes.label: Inspector Barnes
memes.description: A Scotland Yard inspector described as the Yard's authority on nautical matters, consulted by Inspector French during the Cheyne Mystery investigation.

memes.label: "Inspector French"
memes.description: "Scotland Yard Inspector who takes charge of Cheyne's case. A methodical, genial, and highly competent detective whose systematic approach contrasts with Cheyne's impulsive amateur sleuthing."

memes.label: Inspector French Takes Charge
memes.description: Inspector French of Scotland Yard formally takes charge of the Cheyne mystery investigation, marking the transition from amateur sleuthing to professional police work.

memes.label: Chief Inspector Mitchell
memes.description: A Chief Inspector at Scotland Yard who supervised aspects of the Cheyne Mystery investigation and authorized French's trip to Belgium.

memes.label: "Joan Merrill"
memes.description: "Joan Merrill is a key ally of Maxwell Cheyne in The Cheyne Mystery, whose abduction drives the latter half of the investigation."

memes.label: "Kingswear"
memes.description: "A village on the eastern bank of the River Dart in Devon, opposite Dartmouth, serving as a key transit point in the novel's Dart estuary chase sequence."

memes.label: "Laurel Lodge"
memes.description: "A house on Hopefield Avenue occupied by James Dangle, central to the conspiracy and the burglary investigation"

memes.label: Mr. Lavender
memes.description: Co-founder of Horton and Lavender's Private Detective Agency, deceased before the events of the story.

memes.label: "Tom Lewisham"
memes.description: "A member of Blessington's gang who participated in the surveillance of Maxwell Cheyne during the train pursuit from Dartmouth to London."

memes.label: "Liverpool Street"
memes.description: "London railway terminus serving as departure point for Harwich and continental boat trains in The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: "London"
memes.description: "London serves as the central hub of investigation in The Cheyne Mystery, home to Scotland Yard and the setting for crucial developments including the pursuit of Dangle."

memes.label: Lord Hull
memes.description: The elderly and wealthy present Baron Hull, whose title and fortune Arnold Price was believed to be heir to.

memes.label: "Maxwell Cheyne"
memes.description: "Protagonist of The Cheyne Mystery, a twenty-nine-year-old former naval officer and amateur yachtsman drawn into a criminal conspiracy."

memes.label: Miss Cheyne (Agatha)
memes.description: Maxwell Cheyne's sister (or daughter), known as Agatha, resident of Warren Lodge.

memes.label: Miss Hazelton (Ethel)
memes.description: Ethel Hazelton, the grown-up daughter of Mrs. Hazelton, who assisted in relaying urgent messages during the Warren Lodge crisis.

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: Statistical ranking of the most cross-referenced entries in The Cheyne Mystery wiki, by total backlink count.

memes.label: Mr. Jesse
memes.description: The manager of the Edgecombe Hotel in Plymouth, who assisted Maxwell Cheyne after he was drugged and hired a private detective on the hotel's behalf.

memes.label: Mr. Lowenthal
memes.description: Junior partner of the Merkel & Lowenthal shipping firm in Antwerp, interviewed by Inspector French during his investigation of Dangle's disappearance.

memes.label: Mr. Merkel
memes.description: Head of the Antwerp shipping firm Merkel & Lowenthal, whose vessel the L'Escaut was used by the conspirators to transport gold bullion

memes.label: Mr. Pringle
memes.description: City analyst in Plymouth who conducted the chemical analysis of the drugged coffee, confirming the presence of a drug

memes.label: Mrs. Bryce
memes.description: A fictional name used by Joan Merrill as a pretext when scouting Blessington's house at Earlswood.

memes.label: Mrs. Cheyne
memes.description: Maxwell Cheyne's mother, resident of Warren Lodge, who purchased the estate and maintained the household.

memes.label: Mrs. Hazelton
memes.description: A neighbor of the Cheyne family who owned a telephone and relayed messages between Maxwell Cheyne and Warren Lodge.

memes.label: Mrs. Price
memes.description: Arnold Price's wife, who stood to benefit from the recovered cipher documents alongside other interested parties.

memes.label: Mrs. Sproule
memes.description: The wife of Mr. Sproule of 17 Colton Street, an invalid who provided key witness information to Inspector French.

memes.label: "Newcastle"
memes.description: "Port city in northeast England, mentioned as a shipping destination in The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: "Newton Abbot"
memes.description: "A market town in Devonshire and key railway junction on the Great Western Railway line, featured in the conspirators' train pursuit of Maxwell Cheyne."

memes.label: "Ostend"
memes.description: "A Belgian port city on the North Sea coast, serving as a key transit point for Inspector French's continental investigation in the novel."

memes.label: "Otto Schulz"
memes.description: "Otto Schulz was a German U-boat captain during the First World War whose discovery of a valuable secret — later encoded by Arnold Price — is the hidden treasure at the heart of The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: Paignton
memes.description: Coastal town on Tor Bay in South Devon, one of the possible landing sites searched by Cheyne during his pursuit of the Enid.

memes.label: Places Index
memes.description: Complete index of all locations appearing in The Cheyne Mystery by Freeman Wills Crofts.

memes.label: "Plymouth"
memes.description: "Plymouth is the city where the opening events of The Cheyne Mystery unfold, including Maxwell Cheyne's drugging at the Edgecombe Hotel."

memes.label: "The Plymouth Drugging"
memes.description: "The inciting incident of The Cheyne Mystery -- Maxwell Cheyne is drugged by Blessington at the Edgecombe Hotel in Plymouth, setting the entire adventure in motion."

memes.label: "RMS Maurania"
memes.description: "An eight-thousand-ton ocean liner on which Arnold Price served as third officer and where the pivotal St. John Price incident occurred."

memes.label: Sam Pullar
memes.description: Contact at Lloyd's Record Department who provided Inspector French with crucial shipping data about the sinking of the Silurian

memes.label: "Otto Schulz's Secret"
memes.description: "Otto Schulz's secret -- a discovery made aboard a German U-boat during the Great War -- is the hidden truth behind the cipher and the driving motivation for Dangle's criminal pursuit."

memes.label: "Scotland Yard"
memes.description: "Headquarters of the Metropolitan Police in London, where Inspector French conducts his investigation of Cheyne's case."

memes.label: Sergeant Burnett
memes.description: Police sergeant assigned by Inspector French to surveillance duty at Waterloo Station, who successfully identified the taxi used by the suspects

memes.label: Sergeant Carter
memes.description: A police sergeant at Scotland Yard who assisted Inspector French in searches and investigations during the Cheyne Mystery case.

memes.label: "Sime"
memes.description: "Sime is a criminal associate of James Dangle, serving as the muscle in the Dangle-Sime-Blessington conspiracy in The Cheyne Mystery."

memes.label: "Mr. Speedwell"
memes.description: "Mr. Speedwell is a private inquiry agent from Horton and Lavender's Agency who provides professional investigative support to Maxwell Cheyne."

memes.label: "Speedwell's Intervention"
memes.description: "Mr. Speedwell's intervention marks the moment when professional investigative resources are brought to bear on the Cheyne mystery, shifting the balance of power against Dangle's criminal gang."

memes.label: Mr. Sproule
memes.description: A resident of 17 Colton Street, Putney, who contacted Inspector French with critical witness testimony about a motor-car seen outside Sime's house, helping establish the timeline of Joan Merrill's abduction.

memes.label: "St. John Price"
memes.description: "A diplomat and genealogist whose discoveries about the Hull barony succession drive the novel's central conspiracy — later revealed to be a fabrication of James Dangle's gang."

memes.label: "Susan Dangle"
memes.description: "Susan Dangle is the sister and active accomplice of James Dangle, participating in the conspiracy through impersonation and intelligence-gathering."

memes.label: "The Antwerp Investigation"
memes.description: "Inspector French's investigation in Antwerp, Belgium, tracing James Dangle's movements and uncovering the conspirators' maritime expedition plans."

memes.label: The Conspiracy
memes.description: The organized criminal conspiracy led by James Dangle to seize the Arnold Price cipher and recover Otto Schulz's hidden treasure by any means necessary.

memes.label: "The Dart Chase"
memes.description: "Cheyne's pursuit of Dangle, Price, and Lewisham from Dartmouth across Devon to London after their escape from the L'Escaut."

memes.label: "The Earlswood Escape"
memes.description: "Cheyne's break-in at Dangle's house (Earlswood, Dalton Road, Wembley) to recover the linen tracing, and his dramatic escape when discovered."

memes.label: "The Enid"
memes.description: "The steam launch used by Blessington to kidnap Maxwell Cheyne and hold him prisoner during the conspiracy to obtain Arnold Price's cipher."

memes.label: The Hopefield Avenue Discovery
memes.description: Maxwell Cheyne discovers and investigates the conspirators' hideout on Hopefield Avenue, a key breakthrough in tracing the gang.

memes.label: "The L'Escaut"
memes.description: "The L'Escaut is the tramp steamer that serves as the ultimate destination in The Cheyne Mystery — the vessel linked to the resolution of Schulz's secret and the final confrontation with Dangle."

memes.label: "The L'Escaut Voyage"
memes.description: "The conspirators' maritime expedition aboard the L'Escaut from Antwerp to recover Otto Schulz's sunken gold, with Joan Merrill held captive aboard."

memes.label: The Photograph
memes.description: Key photographic evidence used by both the conspirators and the investigators to document the linen tracing and the Arnold Price cipher.

memes.label: "The Linen Tracing"
memes.description: "The linen tracing is the key physical document in The Cheyne Mystery — a sheet of tracing linen that encodes the solution to the Arnold Price cipher and the location of Schulz's secret."

memes.label: The Treasure
memes.description: The hidden treasure discovered by U-boat captain Otto Schulz during World War I, whose location was encoded in the Arnold Price cipher — the central MacGuffin of the Cheyne mystery.

memes.label: Torquay
memes.description: Coastal resort town in South Devon, landing place of the Enid during the conspirators' escape and key location in Cheyne's pursuit.

memes.label: "Warren Lodge"
memes.description: "Maxwell Cheyne's family home, a small Georgian house on the estuary of the River Dart near Dartmouth, Devonshire."

memes.label: "The Warren Lodge Burglary"
memes.description: "The burglary of Maxwell Cheyne's safe at Warren Lodge, the second crime in the conspiracy to obtain Arnold Price's secret document."

memes.label: Waterloo Station
memes.description: Major London railway terminus used by the criminals to switch vehicles during their escape.

memes.label: Wembley Park
memes.description: A park and railway station in northwest London, where Cheyne and Joan took a train during their investigation.
